fundamentals.name: MP Materials Corp.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MP Materials Corp., together with its subsidiaries, produces rare earth materials in the Western Hemisphere. It operates in two segments, Materials and Magnetics. The Materials segment owns and operates the Mountain Pass Rare Earth Mine and Processing facility located near Mountain Pass, San Bernardino County, California. The Magnetics segment produces magnetic precursor products, including NdPr metal; and manufactures NdFeB permanent magnets. MP Materials Corp. was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Las Vegas, Nevada.

judgment.summary: MP Materials shows strong revenue growth and a solid cash position, but is currently unprofitable with a high forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus by 174.6%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 118.6% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS beat estimate by 174.6% — significant positive surprise.
Total cash $1.74B and current ratio 7.18 — strong liquidity and balance sheet.
Analyst consensus is 'strong_buy' with a mean target of $80.40.
Forward P/E 55.06x, but analyst growth estimates are very high (current FY 228.7%).
negatives: Operating margin -7.9% and Profit margin -20.5% — currently loss-making.
Price/Sales (TTM) 33.02x is high, indicating a premium valuation relative to sales.
Beta of 1.91 suggests higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: MP Materials (MP) stock has seen recent downward pressure despite a new partnership with Apple, with valuation and geopolitical factors also influencing expectations. While the company reported a significant earnings beat in its last quarter, with EPS of $0.03 versus an estimated -$0.04, its operating and profit margins remain negative. The stock's performance is being weighed against broader industry trends and strategic investments by major figures in defense and technology.
